verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (human kallikrein-2) is a secreted serine protease expressed predominantly in prostate epithelial cells. Like other kallikreins (KLK1, KLK3/PSA), it is synthesized as a preproenzyme, processed through the secretory pathway, and released into the extracellular milieu (prostatic fluid, seminal plasma, blood). It has a signal peptide and prodomain but no transmembrane domain, GPI signal, or known outer-leaflet lipidation. Pre-`no` checks: (1) No documented cell-surface-directed therapeutic targets the KLK2 protein body on cells — diagnostic PSA/KLK2 immunoassays detect circulating soluble protein. (2) Not a TM proligand with sheddase biology; purely secreted. (3) No covalent/wash-resistant TM-partner tethering documented. (4) No minority PM pool from cycling/lysosomal exocytosis documented. (5) No cancer ecto-pool, no cell-death surface display, no activation-induced display, no narrow germline surface expression documented. KLK2 is purely a secreted extracellular enzyme with no stable PM association.
